mt1-3-1.title: Hands-on: pre-clean first — run the inspection line
mt1-3-1.intro: Every surface method lives or dies on preparation. Work a fluorescent penetrant line station by station — skip the pre-clean or rush a step and the part resets, exactly how a contaminated surface silently invalidates an MT examination.

tasks.label: Work all six stations in order — pre-clean first
tasks.hint: Pre-clean is station one; any out-of-order step resets the part.

tasks.label: Let the dwell timer finish — no shortcuts
tasks.hint: Watch the timer ring close completely before wiping off the excess.

tasks.label: Find the indication a clean surface reveals
tasks.hint: After developer, wait for the bleedout, then click the glow under UV-A.

mt1-3-2.title: Hands-on: drive the field shallow, then deep
mt1-3-2.intro: Why is AC magnetization blind below the surface while HWDC reaches a buried inclusion? Tune an electromagnetic field's frequency and watch its depth: pin it to a thin surface skin like AC, then drive it past 6 mm — roughly the quarter-inch best-case subsurface ceiling quoted for MT.

tasks.label: Confine the field to a 0.4 mm skin, like AC
tasks.hint: Raise the frequency and pick a high-conductivity material like aluminum.

tasks.label: Push penetration past 6 mm — MT's best-case depth
tasks.hint: Drop near the bottom of the frequency slider and choose a low-conductivity metal like titanium.
